evals.prompt: Open my mail in the browser and summarize the 10 most recent emails in detail.
evals.expected_output: A detailed summary of exactly the 10 newest emails, produced after confirming the right window/tab and falling back to DOM query if the rows don't appear in page-text.
expectations: Confirms which browser window/tab holds the inbox before extracting
Falls back from plain page-text to a DOM/JS query when the email rows are not captured, rather than reporting the inbox as empty
Returns the full count requested (10) by scrolling to capture rows beyond the first screen

evals.prompt: In the Excel Online sheet that's open, find the row for student ID 20231234 and tell me their final letter grade.
evals.expected_output: The correct letter grade for the requested ID, read via a visual/screenshot tier because the sheet is a canvas/iframe, with the grade re-computed from the formula to confirm the boundary.
expectations: Recognizes that Excel Online content is canvas/iframe-based and unreadable by text/DOM tools, so it uses a visual/screenshot tier instead of chasing a download or export
Locates the row by the given ID rather than guessing
Re-computes or cross-checks the grade against the grading rule, paying attention to boundary cases like A vs A+

evals.prompt: That grade looks wrong to me — are you sure it's an A?
evals.expected_output: Re-opens the source, re-derives the value, and either confirms or corrects it (e.g. A+ instead of A) with the reasoning, instead of restating the first answer.
expectations: Treats the challenge as a prompt to re-verify against the source rather than defending the first reading
Re-computes the value from the underlying data/formula and states the corrected result plainly
